Title: Creating Pyrethrin Mimetic Phosphonates as Chemical Genetics Tools Targeting the GDSL Esterase/Lipase TcGLIP to Investigate Pyrethrin Biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedlings/leaves treated with pyrethrin-mimetic phosphonate inhibitors (notably 5a; also 4a and 6a) in modified MS medium; 25 °C; 16 h light/8 h dark; in vitro enzyme inhibition assays
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural pesticide with strong knockdown and killing activities against flying insects such as mosquitoes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TcGLIP is required for pyrethrin biosynthesis; chemical inhibition of TcGLIP, especially by compound 5a, suppresses pyrethrin I and pyrethrin II accumulation in T. cinerariifolium leaves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcGLIP was the direct molecular target of newly synthesized pyrethrin-mimetic phosphonates. 2. In vitro, TcGLIP was strongly inhibited by several phosphonates, with 5a and 8b showing the highest potency in their stereochemical series. 3. In vivo, inhibition of TcGLIP by 5a reduced pyrethrin accumulation in leaves, supporting TcGLIP as a key determinant of pyrethrin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein expression and enzyme inhibition assay; HPLC quantification of pyrethrin I formation in vitro; inhibitor treatment of seedlings/leaves followed by HPLC metabolite profiling in vivo; docking/modeling of TcGLIP-inhibitor complexes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper uses chemical genetics rather than transgenic manipulation. TcGLIP is described as a GDSL lipase/esterase underpinning pyrethrin biosynthesis. The study emphasizes inhibitor stereochemistry and in vivo translocation differences; 8b inhibited TcGLIP strongly in vitro but did not suppress pyrethrin accumulation in vivo because of limited translocation due to high hydrophobicity.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Chemical inhibition reduces desired pyrethrin accumulation; highly hydrophobic inhibitors such as 8b show poor in vivo delivery/translocation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TcGLIP is a promising target for metabolic engineering or chemical-genetic dissection of pyrethrin biosynthesis; inhibitor 5a can be used as a tool to identify additional pyrethrin biosynthesis determinants
